Suppose that $f : \mathbb{R} \to \mathbb{R}$ is infinitely differentiable and
satisfies both of the following properties:

\begin{enumerate}[(a)]

\item $f(1) = 2$,
\item If $\alpha, \beta$ are real numbers satisfying $\alpha^2 + \beta^2 = 1$,
      then $f(\alpha x) f(\beta x) = f(x)$ for all $x$.

\end{enumerate}

Find $f(x)$. Guesswork will not be accepted.
